title: The Throne Room
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 22. Opens at the palace service entrance and closes in custody after the Imperial Chamber engagement.
transport: Stealth movement on foot through service corridors -> live combat across the Imperial Chamber -> escorted arrest on foot into custody.
immediateObjective: Reach Frostmourne, buy three to five seconds, and break the blade's hold before Arthas and the room around him finish the kingdom.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Jaina's perception only
No interior access to Sylvanas, Arthas, or late witnesses
No clean solution — the best available outcome is still partial and politically damning
Antonidas and Uther remain margin presences, not room-side rescue

povKnowsAtOpen: The anti-blade framework is real: frost for rhythm, arcane for breach, fire for transformation.
Sylvanas is the combat partner who will buy the seconds the cast needs.
Antonidas and two Kirin Tor magi got the pair to a side service lane and cannot come any farther without collapsing stealth.
Arthas is already in the palace and Uther cannot reach the king by formal means.
Jaina's reserves are critically low, but her sensitivity is sharp enough to track the blade directly through walls.
Cyndia and the rangers must hold the exit lane because the room itself is all the plan can currently afford.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Terenas is still alive when they reach the chamber.
Whether Uther or Antonidas intersects them again before the room closes in a way that matters.
Whether the seam stays usable once the spell is under live pressure rather than cave observation.
What the political shape of survival looks like even if the spell works.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No clean or total solution; the best possible outcome here is still imperfect.
No guarantee that any outsider witnesses the murder itself.
No promise that breaking Arthas's hold on the blade frees the trapped souls inside it or cuts the deeper tether north.
No narrator access to Sylvanas's or Arthas's interiority.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Terenas dies before S&J can stop the murder.
Jaina's three-school sequence ruptures Arthas's bearer-bond and scars Frostmourne's field from the inside without cutting the deeper tether to Ner'zhul.
Sylvanas's second arrow kills Arthas through the seam Jaina forces open permanently.
The blade remains active, unheld, and full of trapped souls after the victory.
Antonidas and the Kirin Tor cell never reach the chamber in time to become useful witnesses.
The room's evidence profile is catastrophic for anyone trying to prove innocence later.
The palace guard arrests the women who just saved the kingdom because the room reads the wrong way to every late witness.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: The framework works on paper and in her head; now it has to survive a real room, a real king, and a blade close enough to hurt simply by being near it.
pov.surfaceObjective: Reach casting range, hold the sequence steady, and convert the seam from theory into victory before Sylvanas's bought seconds run out.
pov.pressureSource: Near-empty reserves, the blade's proximity, Arthas and Terenas in the same room, and the knowledge that Uther may be close enough to help and still too far away to matter.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Jaina still wants the spell to produce a clean answer if she executes it perfectly, even though the room has already made cleanliness impossible.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can accept an imperfect save — king dead, souls trapped, blade still active, deeper tether uncut — if imperfection is the only form survival can take.
pov.forcedChoice: Cast now without witness or reinforcement, or wait and let the blade keep feeding.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina goes from theorist and tracker to the person who scars the blade from the inside and survives just long enough to see the cost.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 24 opens in cells and chains with the spell successful, the evidence damning, and the blade still whispering overhead.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Combat partner, terrain-maker in a room with almost no terrain, and the body holding the death knight's attention while Jaina works.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina reads Sylvanas through movement, wound timing, positioning, and the way the Ranger-General keeps putting herself where the blade will have to answer her first.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. The price Sylvanas pays is visible only through drain, injury, gait change, and timing.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Prince-shaped wielder of the blade rather than a reachable version of the man Jaina knew.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina reads him through old memory, live resonance, and the visible absence of any hesitation inside him.
throughPov.interactionRule: He is never granted interior justification. The room gives Jaina sight, not access.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Peak — every learned tell and shared rhythm compressed into one room and one engagement.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership proves itself capable of doing the impossible and also proves that impossible victories still come with ruin attached.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the fight, the cast, and the second arrow prove the pair. Do not narrate "perfect trust" ahead of the evidence.

entries.rule: Hold service-passage exit lanes and do not enter the throne-room core unless collapse forces it. Their off-room position matters for later arrest logic.

entries.character: Antonidas and two Kirin Tor magi
entries.rule: Present in the palace margins only. They can keep one side lane from closing and still never make the room in time to matter legally.
Present in the palace and spiritually certain, but never in the room when the murder happens. The near-miss must remain razor-sharp.
Read as living armor from a glance and blade-echoes from Jaina's deeper sense. They must feel like proof the palace has already been colonized from the inside.
Living, frightened, and late. They interpret the room from evidence they can actually see.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 24 opens in dungeon cells and tribunal procedure, with the blade in custody above them and no useful witness to the murder itself.

scenes.title: The Infiltration
scenes.sceneFunction: Get the pair into the palace, prove the interior is already compromised, and spend Uther's credibility just outside the room that needs it most.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The plan exists, the palace is open, and the room ahead matters more than any outer defense.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep service doors, lye-smell, corridor geometry, and resonance spikes concrete so the palace feels inhabited and corrupted at once.

enrichment.detail: Lordaeron's palace service passages canonically support kitchens, staff traffic, and narrow logistics corridors behind the ceremonial rooms; the throne-room flanks are reachable through servant-side doors while peacetime security stays focused on public halls, not service flow.

beats.goal: Enter the palace unseen and place Cyndia's team on the extraction route.
beats.conflict: Every second spent moving carefully is another second the blade can use; every careless sound risks collapsing the room before the plan starts.

outcome.summary: The service door gets them inside cleanly and the split works, but the moment Jaina crosses into the passages the blade's proximity starts exacting a bodily price.

beats.valueShift: outside the palace -> inside the machine
newOnPageInformation: The service lanes are unguarded because peacetime habit still rules the palace margins.
Cyndia takes the exit route with two rangers while one ranger stays nearer the pair.
Jaina's channel gets louder enough indoors that the bread-taste becomes almost unmanageable.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the service split is enough if the room implodes.
How many undead nodes actually stand between them and the chamber.
sensoryAnchors: kitchen grease
lye in narrow stone corridors
service door wood worn smooth by years of trays and carts
Cyndia's last sweep before she disappears around the corner
beats.transitionIntent: question-pressure: once the pair are inside, the chapter can let the deeper sense catch the guards before the eyes do.

beats.goal: Remove the undead chamber guards without alerting Arthas.
beats.conflict: The sentries look living at a distance, and if either gets a signal off the entire plan dies outside the room.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas and Jaina clear the guards cleanly and silently, but their presence proves the palace is already seeded with the dead while the court keeps pretending nothing is wrong.

beats.valueShift: inside the machine -> proof of inner colonization
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can distinguish blade-echoes from living bodies through resonance alone.
Sylvanas can read the same wrongness from motion and stillness even without magic.
Arthas has placed undead sentries inside the king's own house while the living staff continue routine work.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the dead sentries are isolated nodes or part of a responsive chain.
Whether the service door concealment still holds once the chamber opens.
sensoryAnchors: halberds held too still in torchlight
armor and meat hitting stone without any human cry
bread-taste roaring louder with the throne-room door only paces away
beats.transitionIntent: witness-pressure: once the room is close enough to smell, the chapter can spend Uther's credibility at the worst possible moment.

beats.emotion: Recognition, urgency, and the knowledge that the best witness in the kingdom is finally here and still not here enough.
beats.dilemma: Use Uther as validation and lose the room's timing, or let him go get the Order and accept that his absence from the murder will later destroy his usefulness in court.
beats.decision: S&J go on. Uther runs for the Silver Hand.
beats.valueShift: proof of inner colonization -> the witness walks away
newOnPageInformation: Uther confirms the same wrongness in the palace with paladin senses.
He is already a discarded voice in a building that should have listened to him days ago.
The pair need seconds in the chamber now, not thirty more minutes of moral correctness.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Uther returns in time to see anything that matters.
Whether the Silver Hand can affect the room after the blade feeds again.
sensoryAnchors: plate armor humming lightly through the corridor stones
Uther's voice rough from repeated failed audience attempts
the bread-taste standing in for a clock no one else can hear
beats.transitionIntent: lock-in: once Uther leaves, the pair own the room alone.

scenes.title: The Murder
scenes.sceneFunction: Reveal the room, let the murder happen, and prove immediately that conventional force is not enough.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The pair are at the service entrance with no credible witness in the room.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep dome, marble, throne platform, crown, and resonance flood physical so the murder lands as space and sensation instead of only plot.

enrichment.detail: The Imperial Chamber is canonically white marble under a high dome with an octagonal window, concentric floor designs, the golden seal of Lordaeron, curtains, chandeliers, and spectator balconies. That ceremony-first architecture gives Jaina light and echo but almost no forgiving combat terrain.

beats.goal: Enter the chamber unseen long enough to read positions and locate the seam at close range.
beats.conflict: The blade's immediate proximity opens Jaina's channel all the way and starts damaging her before the fight even begins.

outcome.summary: The pair get concealment behind curtain and column, and Jaina confirms the seam is real and reachable at close range, but the nosebleed starts the moment the room opens to her senses.

beats.valueShift: the witness walks away -> the room becomes live
newOnPageInformation: Terenas is still alive when they reach the chamber.
Frostmourne's seam is exactly where Jaina found it in the cave.
The room is every bit as tactically hostile as Sylvanas expected — open, echoing, ceremony architecture, no forgiving cover.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the pair can move before Arthas kills the king.
Whether any cast is possible once the blade starts fully broadcasting.
sensoryAnchors: white sunlight pouring through the octagonal dome window
golden seal of Lordaeron on polished marble
warm blood from Jaina's first nosebleed under the blade's full signal
beats.transitionIntent: deadline-pressure: once the room is read, the chapter can stop delaying the murder.

beats.goal: Prevent the murder if there is still a heartbeat left to prevent.
beats.conflict: They are one beat too late, and the first perfect arrow proves the blade can simply erase ordinary killing logic.

outcome.summary: Terenas dies, and the first arrow that would end any living target only proves that Arthas now lives inside a different ruleset.

beats.valueShift: the room becomes live -> concealment dies with the king
newOnPageInformation: Arthas kills Terenas with no hesitation and no visible human struggle.
Frostmourne feeds visibly through the murder.
Sylvanas's perfect throat shot fails and reveals the pair's position at once.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any conventional damage matters now except as distraction.
How much stronger the blade becomes after consuming a king.
sensoryAnchors: crown ringing on marble and rolling in the sun
dark blood in Arthas's throat wound closing instead of acting like blood should
one short line from Arthas flattening fatherhood into procedure
beats.transitionIntent: plan-pressure: once the first arrow fails, the chapter can spend the actual answer.

scenes.title: The Fight
scenes.sceneFunction: Let Sylvanas buy the seconds, Jaina force the seam open, and the partnership win in the only way this room still permits.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The king is dead, concealment is gone, and the only remaining answer is the plan itself.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep columns, frost, drain, timing, blood loss, and the blade's cycle active so the cast feels earned and precarious.

enrichment.detail: Frostmourne canonically scars the spirit, traps souls, and can remake the living into mindless undead; keeping the blade active and hungry after the bearer-channel fails tracks established lore better than a neat magical shutdown would. The local story docs already frame the throne-room cast as damage to the local conduit, not total destruction of the weapon.
enrichment.source: stories/the-plague-road/plans/throne-room-mechanism.md; Warcraft Wiki: Frostmourne; Warcraft Wiki: Frostmourne Cavern

beats.goal: Put Arthas fully on Sylvanas and create Jaina's approach lane behind him.
beats.conflict: Frostmourne's consuming field starts eating Sylvanas the moment she closes, and the chamber offers almost no forgiving terrain.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets Arthas moving and facing forward, but every second of success visibly drains the body Jaina is depending on.

beats.valueShift: concealment dies -> the bought window begins
newOnPageInformation: Bow work now exists only to steer Arthas's attention, not to kill him.
The columns matter because the open floor offers Sylvanas nothing else.
The consuming field drains warmth and speed in real time instead of as abstract threat.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the bought window lasts long enough for all three schools.
What happens if Sylvanas's speed margin collapses before Jaina reaches the seam.
sensoryAnchors: column stone cracking under Frostmourne's impact
cold thickening across the floor in a visible radius
blood on Sylvanas's forearm staying cold as it runs
beats.transitionIntent: timing-pressure: once the positioning is won, the chapter can focus entirely on keeping the seam visible.

beats.goal: Hold the frost synchronization long enough for the seam to stay trackable under live combat.
beats.conflict: Jaina has to maintain exact rhythm while Sylvanas takes hits and the room keeps trying to break concentration apart.

outcome.summary: The frost lock holds and the seam stays visible, but Sylvanas takes a real wound and the survival margin narrows sharply.

beats.valueShift: the bought window begins -> the seam is actionable
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can tune herself to the blade's cycle under pressure, not just in post-fight analysis.
Sylvanas taking a hit is almost enough to break the cast before it starts.
The field's weakest lane is behind Arthas, exactly where the plan needed it to be.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Jaina has enough left to complete the full sequence.
Whether Sylvanas can keep Arthas interested for the remaining seconds.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's cold overlay distinct from Frostmourne's cold
Sylvanas hitting marble with one knee and rising anyway
the seam pulsing like a tiny gap between hammer blows
beats.transitionIntent: all-in: once the window is real, the chapter can spend every last reserve on the three-school cast.

beats.goal: Complete the sequence — arcane through the seam, fire through the breached bearer-channel, and break Arthas's clean hold on the blade without detonating its deeper binding.
beats.conflict: The cast demands all remaining reserves and offers no clean way out if it works.

outcome.summary: The spell succeeds and ruptures Arthas's bearer-channel, but the price is Jaina dropping to the marble almost emptied out of usable strength while the deeper tether still hums beneath what she just damaged.

beats.valueShift: the seam is actionable -> the blade is wounded from within
newOnPageInformation: The binding stack has layers; the local bearer-channel can be burnt out without shattering the deeper tether.
Frostmourne's pull north remains, but its clean local conduit is scarred and unstable.
The seam becomes a permanent flaw once the bearer-channel burns through.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether a still-tethered blade leaves a lethal wielder standing once the local conduit fails.
What a wounded, unheld Frostmourne means for the souls already trapped inside the weapon.
sensoryAnchors: arcane force crossing the seam as a colorless punch through the gap
fire turning the blade's inner channels amber from within
Jaina's face hitting cold marble when the cast finishes
beats.transitionIntent: kill-shot: once Arthas loses the clean bond, the chapter can let Sylvanas use the opening she just bought.

beats.goal: Kill Arthas through the permanent flaw in the blade's defenses.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas is drained, wounded, and down to an emergency recurve and one remaining arrow.

outcome.summary: The arrow passes the seam, takes the eye, and drops Arthas where the first shot could not.

beats.valueShift: the blade is wounded from within -> the wielder falls
newOnPageInformation: The permanent flaw matters immediately, not just in theory.
Sylvanas's reserve weapon and final arrow are enough once the blade can no longer fully close around them.
Arthas dies like a puppet cut loose rather than like a prince receiving a noble ending.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the room is safer now that the wielder is dead.
Whether the blade remains merely dangerous or actively hungry without a hand.
sensoryAnchors: small recurve bow instead of longbow elegance
arrow slipping through a gap the field can no longer seal
blade and body hitting marble as two separate final sounds
beats.transitionIntent: aftershock: once Arthas falls, the chapter can show what victory actually looks like in a room with Frostmourne still humming.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beats 6-9 form a four-beat scene run without sequel relief. Keep each beat's local cost and timing distinct.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.sceneFunction: Reveal the imperfect nature of the victory and flip the heroes into criminals in a single room-reading.
castInScene: later Uther and Silver Hand off-beat
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas is down and the blade no longer rides cleanly through him.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep frost, soul-echoes, doors, and late witnesses concrete so the arrest logic feels brutal rather than arbitrary.

enrichment.detail: The ceremonial architecture is built to produce one dominant visual story: white floor, dead king, dead prince, foreign intruders, and the blade at center. Late witnesses and guards are therefore primed to read the chamber as murder aftermath, not as a fight they did not see begin.

beats.emotion: Awe, horror, and the knowledge that the spell worked without solving everything trapped inside the sword.
beats.dilemma: Survive the burst long enough to understand what an unheld Frostmourne is doing before the doors open on the wrong story.
beats.decision: Endure the burst, stay down, and read the weapon's new state instead of turning the room into a second blind panic.
beats.valueShift: the wielder falls -> the weapon still speaks
newOnPageInformation: The wounded blade erupts in frost and resonance the moment it hits the floor.
Trapped soul-echoes flash visibly, including Terenas's freshest imprint.
The transformation killed Arthas's clean conduit but did not free the dead or cut the deeper tether.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the room can contain the weapon's new state.
How anyone outside this chamber will ever understand what just happened.
sensoryAnchors: frost racing through floor grooves like veins
faces above the blade for two heartbeats and no longer
the room temperature dropping past ordinary winter into something with no season
beats.transitionIntent: door-pressure: once the blade's new voice fills the room, the chapter can open the doors and let the evidence read wrong.

beats.emotion: The horror of being seen in the wrong context after doing the right thing.
beats.dilemma: Fight exhausted living guards while the blade still hums, or surrender and let the room's evidence lock the pair into a story they did not write.
beats.decision: Surrender. Not because the accusation is fair, but because there is nothing left to spend on explaining the room to people who arrived late.
beats.valueShift: the weapon still speaks -> victory turns into custody
newOnPageInformation: Guards see two foreigners, two dead royals, and a cursed sword on the floor, and that is enough to decide the shape of events.
No one in the first wave dares touch Frostmourne.
Uther and the Silver Hand arrive too late to witness the murder and early enough only to find the aftermath.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any legal process in the palace can hold the blade safely.
Whether any witness can now stop the tribunal logic from forming.
sensoryAnchors: bronze doors opening on the chamber's freeze
spearpoints lowering toward two women too exhausted to explain anything well
frost on Jaina's cheek as hands bind her where she fell
beats.transitionIntent: carry-forward: Chapter 24 opens in cells and tribunals with the room's evidence already doing its work.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beat 10 originally held reaction without a true decision. The validated plan normalizes the beat around endurance-as-choice so the sequel contract stays intact.

factsLockedForChapter24: Terenas is dead and his soul remains trapped inside Frostmourne.
Arthas is dead from Sylvanas's second arrow through the permanent seam.
Frostmourne is no longer riding through Arthas but remains tethered to Ner'zhul and far from inert.
Jaina is unusually sensitive to Frostmourne's broadcasts after the cast, and at moments the pulse seems to hitch as if the wounded blade notices the mage who scarred it.
Uther is not an eyewitness to the murder.
The tribunal will inherit a room whose evidence profile overwhelmingly favors the wrong story.
factsNotYetEarned: Any acquittal or meaningful legal fairness
Any public recognition that the pair saved the kingdom
Any immediate rescue by Silver Hand, Kirin Tor, or Silvermoon

warnings: Beats 1-5 darken almost continuously before the fight gives the chapter any upward movement; differentiate corridor dread, witness loss, room shock, and the murder itself.
Beats 6-9 are four consecutive scene beats without a sequel. The fight needs sharp internal separation so the reader can track cost, timing, and the seam.
Beats 6-9 all move upward from the pair's perspective (window -> seam -> wounded blade -> fallen wielder). Vary the local cost texture so the win does not flatten into one long lift.
Scene-beat outcomes skew heavily toward yes-but (beats 1, 2, 4, 6, 7, 8), with only one no-and and one clean yes. Keep the compromises explicit.
Beat 10's sequel logic was normalized into a real endurance decision so the aftermath still has reaction -> choice structure before the arrest.
